local_hosting_expected_effect: Minimal -- already near floor (median 0.5s), nothing to gain.
Minimal -- already near floor (median 0.6s), nothing to gain.
Moderate -- removes rate-limit fallback rotations (~12% of steps); typical-case (median) latency already fast and would barely change.
Partial -- removes shared-tenant queueing/network RTT, but genuine 70B compute remains; expect faster, not free (median already 43% of the 60s interval even without errors).

Large -- endpoint is effectively dead (80%+ of steps hit HTTP 504); median decision time alone (80s) already exceeds the 60s control interval, so almost all overhead here is provider-side, not compute.
